$comment: Per-gate field shape, embedded as a reference inside gates.json. Copy a shape into the `gates:` array, fill in the fields, then run `./orchestration/gates/gate-check.sh --list` to confirm it's discoverable. See docs/USER-GUIDE.md §Phase 4 for the authoring workflow.
_id_doc: Stable id; convention is `G.<wave-number>-<short-name>` (e.g. G.wave-1-slots). Manager/captain reference this id in dispatch briefs.
_description_doc: Human-readable purpose of the gate. Surfaces in `gate-check.sh --list` output.
_requires_doc: Predecessor track-ids that must report `[complete:<id>]` before this gate is eligible to run. Empty list = always eligible (e.g. trunk-health gates). The manager checks `requires:` against `origin/swarm/*` completion markers before invoking the gate.
_commands_doc: Sequential shell commands. gate-check.sh stops on the first non-zero exit. Typical TypeScript gates chain `pnpm typecheck && pnpm req-lint && pnpm check-track-meta-paths && pnpm test`; wave-specific gates may add `pnpm --filter @charliehealth/<pkg> test:integration` or codegen-current checks.
